symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious severe fatigue, anorexia and mild nausea over 3 weeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The non-specific constitutional prodrome typical of the pre-icteric phase of acute hepatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: New jaundice (yellow skin and sclerae) with dark urine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Icterus with dark (bilirubinuric) urine indicates hepatocellular/cholestatic injury and is the alarm feature demanding urgent workup.
Dark bilirubinuric urine indicates conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia, arguing against unconjugated haemolytic jaundice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum sickness-like prodrome: low-grade fever, migratory polyarthralgia, faint maculopapular trunk rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immune-complex-mediated serum sickness-like syndrome preceding jaundice is characteristic of acute HBV, more so than of hepatitis A or autoimmune hepatitis.
Hepatitis A more often has an abrupt febrile onset without the serum sickness-like prodrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-risk parenteral exposure — unreported occupational needle-stick ~3 months ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A blood-borne exposure ~3 months before onset sits squarely in the HBV incubation window and provides a plausible acquisition route.
Hepatic capsular stretch from an inflamed, enlarged liver produces mild RUQ ache.
RUQ discomfort is shared with biliary pathology; the mild, non-severe character and systemic prodrome favour hepatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatomegaly with tender liver edge and splenomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender hepatomegaly with mild splenomegaly reflects active hepatitis; a clinician palpation finding the telehealth AI cannot obtain, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent asterixis and no confusion (normal mentation) on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of asterixis and normal mentation argue against current hepatic encephalopathy; a clinician assessment the AI cannot reliably perform remotely, hence urgent in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Liver function tests (bilirubin, ALT/AST, ALP, albumin)

investigation_details.expected_result: Hepatitic pattern with markedly raised transaminases and elevated bilirubin (values not available in telehealth and not to be fabricated)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity of hepatocellular injury and cholestasis remains unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: LFTs objectively confirm and grade hepatocellular injury; the first-line urgent blood test.

symptoms.symptom_name: Viral hepatitis serology (HBsAg, Anti-HBc IgM, HBV DNA)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Hepatitis B serology and viral load
investigation_details.expected_result: HBsAg positive with Anti-HBc IgM positive would confirm acute HBV (presumed pending confirmation; result not available via telehealth and not to be fabricated)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific aetiology and infectivity remain unconfirmed and public-health notification cannot be completed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HBsAg with Anti-HBc IgM is diagnostic of acute HBV infection — the confirmatory test the AI must arrange, not assert.

investigation_details.expected_result: A prolonged INR would indicate impaired hepatic synthetic function and evolving fulminant failure (value not available via telehealth and not to be fabricated)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fulminant hepatic failure cannot be excluded; INR is the key severity marker.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Coagulopathy (raised INR) is a defining feature of acute liver failure and drives disposition.
Absence of blood in vomit or stool argues against active GI bleeding/coagulopathic haemorrhage at present, though it must be safety-netted.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Serum sickness-like prodrome preceding icterus after parenteral exposure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The triad of a prodromal serum sickness-like illness, subsequent jaundice, and a high-risk parenteral exposure within the incubation window is the characteristic clinical signature of acute HBV.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades hepatocellular injury; first-line urgent blood test for new jaundice.
Assesses hepatic synthetic function and is the key marker for excluding fulminant hepatic failure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral hepatitis serology (HBsAg, Anti-HBc IgM, HBV DNA)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes the specific aetiology and infectivity and enables notifiable-disease reporting and contact management.
Assesses liver/biliary architecture and helps exclude biliary obstruction if the biochemical pattern is mixed; not first-line before bloods.
No evidence of bacterial infection; antibiotics are inappropriate for acute viral hepatitis and add hepatotoxic/interaction risk.
